reasoning: The challenge features creative multi-layer defenses (HMAC integrity gate, PBKDF2-derived key, X9.23 padding, UTF-16BE encoding, statistical jitter on cycle_count) and excellent flag isolation, but has a critical design flaw: the HMAC integrity check on /command/verify requires INTEGRITY_KEY derived from the unknown SECRET_KEY, making the padding oracle unreachable by any attacker. The intended solution describes HTTP 500/400 status code differentiation that doesn't exist in the code — the actual oracle signal is the cycle_count value returned on 200, not distinct error codes. Additionally, crypto_utils.py is imported nowhere and uses PKCS7 padding while the challenge uses X9.23, suggesting leftover/inconsistent code. The defense layers are ironically too effective, likely rendering the challenge unsolvable as implemented.
